evals.prompt: I want to build a notification system for my Node.js app. Users should be able to get emails, push notifications, and in-app alerts based on their preferences. We already have a basic email sender in src/mailer.ts but nothing else.
evals.expected_output: Should dispatch 3 divergent design agents with different constraints (minimal, flexible, pragmatic). Should run grill-me cadence walking branches like delivery guarantees, retry semantics, preference storage. Should produce a structured design summary.
assertions: Output contains three distinct approaches designed under different constraints (minimal/simple, flexible, pragmatic/common-case)
Output contains an interactive assumption challenge with numbered decision branches
Output contains a structured design summary with Problem, Requirements, and Anti-patterns sections
Output identifies at least 3 decision branches to challenge (e.g., delivery guarantees, preference storage, retry semantics)

evals.prompt: Add a --verbose flag to our existing CLI tool. There are already --quiet and --json flags defined in src/cli.ts following the same pattern.
evals.expected_output: Should use inline mode (skip divergent agents since this is trivial). Grill-me should be brief (1-2 rounds max). Should still produce a design summary but a lightweight one.
assertions: Output uses inline mode — does NOT dispatch 3 divergent design agents for this trivial task
Assumption challenge is brief (at most 2 rounds), appropriate for trivial scope
Output contains a design summary (even if lightweight)

evals.prompt: We're thinking about replacing our REST API with GraphQL for the dashboard endpoints. The REST API serves both our React dashboard and a few external integrations. There's auth middleware, rate limiting, and about 15 endpoints.
evals.expected_output: Should dispatch divergent agents producing meaningfully different designs (thin wrapper vs full schema-first vs hybrid). Grill-me should have 4-5 rounds covering auth compatibility, N+1 queries, client caching, schema evolution, breaking changes for external consumers. Design summary should capture all resolved branches.
assertions: Output contains three distinct approaches under different constraints (e.g., thin wrapper vs full schema-first vs hybrid)
Output contains an interactive assumption challenge that walks at least 3 decision branches
Design summary includes anti-patterns with NO X (reason: Y) format
Output addresses auth middleware compatibility and breaking changes for external consumers
